review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control SC-12(2) focusing on cryptographic key management. They map to Kubernetes features related to secrets and etcd encryption. However, the function 'k8s_secret_rotation_policy_configured' could be more descriptive to indicate what constitutes a pass/fail condition. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but the description could be improved for clarity.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_secret_rotation_policy_configured' to 'k8s_secret_rotation_policy_implemented' to better indicate action and compliance.
title: Cryptographic Key Establishment And Management - Cryptographic key establishment and management | symmetric keys produce, control
step1_reasoning: The control SC-12(2) focuses on the establishment and management of cryptographic keys, specifically symmetric keys. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to the management of secrets and etcd encryption. Ensuring that secrets are encrypted at rest and that etcd encryption is enabled are direct mappings to this control. Additionally, implementing a secret rotation policy aligns with key management practices. TLS for etcd ensures secure transmission of keys.
